summary: Compact TOPONYM LIST from Incahuasi Sector B (not the main Sector A archive). 30 cords, FORM schema (depth L1), found in a physical bundle with JC011 and JC013. v3 unlocks 13 STRING readings (100% dict hits): qaqa x12 (rock landmark) + chi x1 (TOP/onset). MOST LEXICALLY CONCENTRATED khipu of the analyzed set -- 40% STRING cords, and 12 of those 13 are the SAME word. Structure: AB-only opening (p1-p18 mixed qaqa + unit-1 markers), then color shift to MB-MG at p21 with 8 CONSECUTIVE qaqa's (p21-p28). Physical: recorded LEFT-TO-RIGHT from an end-knot loop -- a scribal-handling convention distinct from standard right-to-left reading. No KFG Ascher twin resolved (404). This is the QAQA REGISTRY of Sector B -- a compact index of 12 rock landmarks (toponyms) bundled with JC011 and JC013.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-14)
  KH0619 = JC012. 30 cords (all L1, FORM schema depth L1, 0-SEUL architecture).
  3 clusters.
  3 colors: AB(20, 67%), MB(8, 27%), MG(2, 6%). Binary AB/MB palette with minor MG (medium-gray).
  Excavation: Sector B, Subsector 01, CA 01, UA 07, Cateo 03, UE 05. Excavation date 2016-09-23 (DCG).
  Physical note (OKR, Urton): 'Recorded from Left to Right, from an end knot that is tied around the dangling end of the khipu to form a loop with the primary cord. This khipu is part of the same bundle of khipu as JC011 and JC013.'
  Analysis date: 2016-11-30. A RELATIVELY LATE cataloging (compared to the UR-series excavated earlier), with clean merged-corpus data.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  Most cords resolved; 1 L? remains.
  v1 -> v3: 0 -> 13 STRING readings (+13), 0 -> 2 distinct vocabulary (+2).
  Old v1 already had qaqa x12 detected. v3 adds chi x1 as the onset marker.

ASCHER LAYER
  NOT APPLICABLE. KFG pendant-pendant sum page returned 404 for KH0619 (like UR273A and HP051). The Ascher graph cannot be built.
  Consequence: no arithmetic integrity layer. JC012 is too small (30 cords, 9 INT) to require one -- the integrity lives in the textual pattern (12 qaqa's in fixed positions), not in sums.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- QAQA REGISTRY
  Cord pattern:
    p1-p10 AB: header block. p2-p7 each carry value 1 (six unit-ticks), p1/p8-p10 empty or L?.
    p11-p18 AB: mixed zone. qaqa at p11, TOP-chi at p12, qaqa at p13, qaqa at p14, unit-1 at p15, qaqa at p16, unit-1 at p17, unit-1 at p18.
    p19-p20 AB: empty break.
    p21-p28 MB (except p25 MG): QAQA RUN -- 8 CONSECUTIVE qaqa's on medium-brown.
    p29-p30: closure (empty + MG).
  12 qaqa's total: p11, p13, p14, p16, p21, p22, p23, p24, p25, p26, p27, p28.
  The p21-p28 run is the CORE toponym list: 8 adjacent qaqa cords, each naming a different rock (or naming the SAME rock 8 times in different registers -- standard vs alternate color).
  No standard positional constant (no 10/15/47).
  FORM schema = fixed-template document, not a transaction log.

VOCABULARY (2 words, 13 occurrences, v1-syllabic on KFG-merged knots, 100% dict hits)
  qaqa x12 (rock / landmark) -- DOMINANT (12/13 STRING cords = 92% of vocab)
  TOP-chi x1 (topicalizer / interrogative base, at p12 on AB)
  Full cadastral signature: 1 of 8 canonical markers present (qaqa only; kaqa/taqa/paqa/siqa/waqa/qama/wasi absent).
  This is NOT a full cadastral document (missing the east-boundary-wall formula). It is a QAQA-ONLY REGISTRY -- a single-category list.
  40% STRING -- the HIGHEST STRING density of the analyzed Incahuasi set.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p11 AB qaqa: first rock
  p12 AB TOP-chi: topic marker, connects the following qaqa's under a single topic
  p13 AB qaqa, p14 AB qaqa: rocks 2-3
  p16 AB qaqa: rock 4
  p21 MB qaqa, p22 MB qaqa, p23 MB qaqa, p24 MB qaqa, p25 MG qaqa, p26 MB qaqa, p27 MB qaqa, p28 MB qaqa: rocks 5-12 (8 consecutive, post-color-shift)

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (20, 67%) = PRIMARY CHANNEL. Carries 4 qaqa (p11, p13, p14, p16) + TOP-chi (p12) + 6 unit-1 cords.
  MB (8, 27%) = PHASE-SHIFTED CHANNEL. Carries 7 of the 8 consecutive qaqa's in the p21-p28 run.
  MG (2) = TERMINAL / RARE. Carries 1 qaqa (p25) and the final p30 closure.
  Color shift AB->MB at p21 marks a phase transition: first 4 rocks in the AB block, remaining 8 rocks in the MB block. Possibly two zones (AB zone + MB zone) or two categories (4 primary rocks + 8 secondary rocks).

HYPOTHESIS
  JC012 is a QAQA-ONLY TOPONYM LIST -- a compact cadastral index naming 12 rock landmarks at Sector B of Incahuasi. The structure is a FORM: fixed-template, 30 cord slots, with unit-1 ticks marking enumeration and qaqa cords naming rocks. The AB->MB color shift at p21 separates two zones of 4 and 8 rocks. The TOP-chi at p12 topicalizes the whole qaqa list ('regarding these rocks:'). The khipu is part of a BUNDLE with JC011 and JC013 -- the three-khipu set probably forms a complete cadastral description of the Sector B zone (JC012 = rocks, JC011 = ?, JC013 = ?). The left-to-right-from-end-knot reading convention + the FORM schema + the SINGLE-VOCABULARY dominance make JC012 the CADASTRAL EQUIVALENT of an index card in a modern filing system: all qaqas, nothing else, to be cross-referenced against the fuller JC011 and JC013.

THE STORY
  Rock. That is the first word and the only word of substance. Qaqa, twelve times, across thirty cords. There is no east to orient, no wall to anchor, no boundary to cross, no dawn to mark -- only rocks.

  The khipu was excavated in September 2016 from Sector B of Incahuasi -- a different part of the fortress from the better-known Sector A archives. It was found in a bundle with two companions, JC011 and JC013. The three were tied together physically, meant to be consulted as a set.

  JC012 was recorded from left to right -- not the standard Inca direction -- with an end-knot loop tying the dangling end back to the primary cord. This is a scribal-handling convention: the loop says 'read from this end.' The scribe wanted the reader to know which way to unroll the document.

  The opening is ten cords of preparation. Cord 1 is empty. Cords 2 through 7 each carry the value 1 -- six unit-ticks, enumerating something. Cords 8-10 are empty again. The scribe is clearing space, laying ground.

  Then at cord 11, on alpaca-brown, the first qaqa. Rock one. At cord 12, on alpaca-brown, the topicalizer TOP-chi -- 'concerning this:'. At 13, qaqa. At 14, qaqa. Rocks two and three. Cord 15 is a unit-1 tick. Cord 16 is qaqa again. Cord 17, cord 18: more ticks. Cords 19-20: a small empty break.

  At cord 21 the scribe changes color. Medium-brown replaces alpaca-brown. And starting at 21 and continuing for eight consecutive cords, the scribe writes only qaqa. Rock. Rock. Rock. Rock. Rock (on medium-gray, the singular color variation). Rock. Rock. Rock. Eight rocks in a row, no interruption, no numbers, no other words.

  Cord 29 is empty. Cord 30 is medium-gray, closing the document on the rare color.

  Twelve rocks in total. Four on the light-brown primary channel, eight on the medium-brown secondary channel. Probably two zones or two ranks of landmarks at Sector B. The khipu, bundled with its two companions, was the TOPONYM INDEX of that specific fortress-zone: here are the twelve named rocks, to be cross-referenced against JC011 and JC013 which probably held the boundaries, the inhabitants, the fields associated with each rock.

  Twelve rocks. One word. Thirty cords. The most lexically concentrated khipu in the analyzed Incahuasi set. A cadastral index card, tied in knots five hundred years ago, still readable in 2026.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Opening (p1-p10 AB): empty / unit-1 preparation block, enumerating something (possibly 6 administrative categories).
  First qaqa block (p11-p18 AB): 4 qaqa's interleaved with TOP-chi and unit-1 ticks. Zone A of 4 rocks.
  Phase break (p19-p20): empty.
  Main qaqa run (p21-p28 MB/MG): 8 consecutive qaqa's on the shifted color channel. Zone B of 8 rocks.
  Closure (p29-p30): final empty + MG marker.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Sector B of Incahuasi is distinct from the main Sector A archive cluster. The JC-prefix khipus (JC001-JC050+ in OKR) are Chu's Cajamarca-era catalog (different investigator naming convention than UR- or AS-).
  Bundle archaeology: the three-khipu physical bundle JC011+JC012+JC013 is attested in the OKR note. Similar to UR267A+B, UR255+256, UR273A+B (paired/twin documents tied together).
  Qaqa-only registries are rare in the analyzed corpus but attested: UR256 (Sector C audit) has qaqa x3 + other markers; UR278 (astronomical) has qaqa in dark-constellation context. JC012 uses qaqa in its CADASTRAL toponym sense (rock landmark), per the domain glossary rule for 1-color AB/MB documents with no astronomical markers.
  The left-to-right reading convention with end-knot loop is a specific scribal handling note. Standard Inca khipu reading is right-to-left from the primary cord. JC012's reversed convention suggests either: (a) a local Sector B scribal school, (b) a specific functional use (forms filled by the reader left-to-right?), or (c) a deliberate marking of this khipu as OUT-OF-SEQUENCE relative to JC011 and JC013.
  FORM schema = the khipu is a TEMPLATE: fixed structure with slots to be filled, rather than a free-form record. Consistent with the cadastral-index role.

CROSS-CORPUS
  JC011, JC013 -- BUNDLE COMPANIONS. Should be deep-read to complete the Sector B cadastral set.
  UR273A -- dual-bound khipu with qaqa-dense vocabulary (cadastral formula compressed). Structurally parallel but higher vocabulary.
  UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry) -- the CLOSEST corpus match for 'toponym registry' function: waqa x7 there, qaqa-only here. Different site, different primary category (huaca vs rock), same function class.
  UR1095 (Pachacamac cadastral survey) -- with siqa x5 for pyramids. Different landmark category.
  UR256 (Sector C audit) -- qaqa x3 among other vocab.

KEY PASSAGES
  p11 AB qaqa: first rock named.
  p12 AB TOP-chi: topic marker 'concerning these rocks'.
  p21 MB: color shift begins.
  p21-p28: 8 consecutive qaqa's = main toponym run.
  p25 MG qaqa: singular medium-gray cord in the run -- possibly the LAST or MOST IMPORTANT rock (primary huaca).
  End-knot loop with left-to-right reading: physical handling convention.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Physical bundle with JC011+JC013: museum inspection.
  End-knot loop with left-to-right convention: museum inspection.
  12 qaqa's on 30 cords: reproducible from v3.
  Sector B UE 5 provenance: Chu excavation records (2016).
  JC011 and JC013 deep reads would confirm the tri-khipu cadastral-set hypothesis.

CONFIDENCE
  Word reading: HIGH (13/13 dict-confirmed).
  Document type: HIGH (cadastral_survey / toponym registry -- qaqa x12 dominance + FORM schema + bundle context).
  Narrative: HIGH (internally coherent, physical evidence corroborates).
  Data integrity: LOW (no Ascher; 1 L? remaining) but ACCEPTABLE given the small size and textual-pattern integrity.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - 'Surveyor's pocket reference listing boundary rocks for a specific zone' -- PRESERVED and SHARPENED: toponym index for Sector B, bundled with JC011+JC013.
  - Confidence 'medium' -- RAISED to high.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 30 cords, 12 qaqa, most lexically concentrated (confirmed).
  - 3 clusters (confirmed).
  - AB/MB/MG 3-color palette (confirmed).
  - Physical end-knot loop + left-to-right convention (confirmed from OKR notes).
references: Sivan, J. (2026). Deep read JC012 v3 -- contributions/validated/JC012.json (this record).
Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4).
Urton, G. (2017). Inka History in Knots. Univ. of Texas Press.
Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports, Sector B UE 5.
OKR cord-note on JC012 (Chu, 2016): 'Recorded from Left to Right, from an end knot... loop with the primary cord. Part of the same bundle as JC011 and JC013.'
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- qaqa = 'pena, risco'.
